description: Create beam elements from an outside source (currently 3D ONLY).

6.0.description: Create beam elements from an outside source.

keywords.name: from-file
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry file s.

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry set named s. The range applies to the list of edges in the geometry set.

keywords.name: from-extruder
keywords.description: Create elements from the extruder set named s. The range applies to the list of edges in the extruder set.

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the two element end-nodes to an existing element as follows. At each end-node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: (1) the ID is given; (2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and (3) the found node is part of an element with ID equal to i.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the elements created to group s. Use of the group logic is described in Group.

keywords.name: group-slot
keywords.description: Assign the group to slot s. If omitted, the group is assigned to the slot named Default.

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f. The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1.

keywords.name: snap
keywords.description: Indicate that the first and last positions of the element(s) created will attempt to “snap” to the location of the nearest structural node (attached to any element type) within a radius of 1/2 the element length. If only one end is snapped in this manner, then the entire line created will be offset by the difference between the original and the snapped position.

7.0.description: Create beam elements from an outside source.

keywords.name: from-extruder
keywords.description: Create elements from the extruder set named s. The range applies to the list of edges in the extruder set.

keywords.name: from-file
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry file s.

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry set named s. The range applies to the list of edges in the geometry set.

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: format
keywords.description: Specify the format of the file. Can only be given with the from-file keyword. This keyword can only be used with the following keywords of the structure beam import command: from-file.

keywords.name: format-dxf
keywords.description: Specify the DXF file format.

keywords.name: format-geom
keywords.description: Specify the geometry file format.

keywords.name: format-stl
keywords.description: Specify the STL file format.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the elements created to group s. Use of the group logic is described in Groups. If not specified, the default group name assigned will be Beam N where N is the ID number assinged to the elements.

keywords.name: group-begin
keywords.description: A group name that will be assigned to the first node of the elements created along a line. The default name assigned will be Beam Begin.

keywords.name: group-end
keywords.description: A group name that will be assigned to the first node of the elements created along a line. The default name assigned will be Beam End.

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the two element end-nodes to an existing element as follows. At each end-node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: (1) the ID is given; (2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and (3) the found node is part of an element with ID equal to i.

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f. The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1.

keywords.name: snap
keywords.description: Indicate that the first and last positions of the element(s) created will attempt to “snap” to the location of the nearest structural node (attached to any element type) within a radius of 1/2 the element length. If only one end is snapped in this manner, then the entire line created will be offset by the difference between the original and the snapped position.

keywords.name: from-sketch
keywords.description: Create elements from Sketch set named s . The range applies to the list of edges in the Sketch set.

keywords.name: from-file
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry file s . The optional format keyword can be used to specify the format of the file. The options are dxf , geom , or stl . [CS: work out what needs to happen here later; formats should have links] If not specified, the format is assumed from the file extension.

keywords.name: from-geometry
keywords.description: Create elements from the geometry set named s . The range applies to the list of edges in the geometry set.

keywords.name: distinct
keywords.description: Eliminate sharing of nodes among created elements. Every element will create a new set of nodes, even if they are at the same location in space and elements share the same ID number. This option is most commonly used with a later structure node join command to create links connecting nodes at the same location in order to apply custom inter-element behavior.

keywords.name: stl
keywords.description: Specify the format of the file. Can only be given with the from-file keyword. This keyword can only be used with the following keywords of the structure beam import command: from-file . Specify the DXF file format. Specify the geometry file format. Specify the STL file format.

keywords.name: group
keywords.description: Assign the elements created to group s . Use of the group logic is described in Groups . If not specified, the default group name assigned will be Beam N where N is the ID number assinged to the elements.

keywords.name: group-begin
keywords.description: A group name that will be assigned to the first node of the elements created along a line. The default name assigned will be Beam Begin .

keywords.name: group-end
keywords.description: A group name that will be assigned to the first node of the elements created along a line. The default name assigned will be Beam End .

keywords.name: id
keywords.description: Specify the ID that will be assigned to all new elements created by this command. If this value is not given, then the next available ID will be used. The ID controls potential joining of the two element end-nodes to an existing element as follows. At each end-node location, a new node is created unless all of the following conditions are fulfilled: (1) the ID is given; (2) an existing node is found “near” the potential new node location; and (3) the found node is part of an element with ID equal to i .

keywords.name: maximum-length
keywords.description: Break the element into segments no greater than length f . The segments setting, if provided, is overridden as needed by this setting.

keywords.name: segments
keywords.description: Break the element into i equal segments, creating new nodes as needed. The default value is 1.

keywords.name: snap
keywords.description: Indicate that the first and last positions of the element(s) created will attempt to “snap” to the location of the nearest structural node (attached to any element type) within a radius of 1/2 the element length. If only one end is snapped in this manner, then the entire line created will be offset by the difference between the original and the snapped position. Note that a new node is created and snapped to the location of the nearest existing node - the element being created does not use an existing node as one of its end nodes. Use struct node join if you want to join the newly created node to the existing node.
